Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendant Mason Paul Wessel To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff?S Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Louis R. Barella Pursuant To Code Civ. Proc. 435.5
Off Calendar May-25-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MASON WESSEL'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's Complaint.

Defendant Mason Paul Wessel's motion to strike punitive damages allegations is denied. Punitives in drunk driving cases is a recurrent issue; plaintiffs and defendants both have their oft-cited authorities. (See, e.g., Taylor v. Sup. Ct. (1979) 24 Cal.3d 89; Dawes v. Sup. Ct. (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 82.) However, even defendants' cases say aggravating circumstances beyond taking the wheel drunk support punitive damages. (Id.) Here, plaintiff pleads that defendant knew "from the outset" he would need to drive after drinking "and proceeded to operate a motor vehicle at high speed and in a reckless manner on Interstate 80." This suffices at the pleading stage.

Prevailing/Moving party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/RBU)
